text: Trocar placement is visible, followed by the docking of the surgical robot. The surgeon performs adhesiolysis to identify the herniated root limb.

A lighted bougie is inserted into the root limb to delineate it from surrounding adhesions, aiding in the mediastinal dissection. The gastrojejunal anastomosis is visibly freed from its mediastinal attachments.
The surgeon performs a posterior cruroplasty, suturing the crura of the diaphragm to reinforce the esophageal hiatus.

The surgeon identifies the Bochdalek hernia defect in the left diaphragm. The omentum and colon are reduced back into the abdominal cavity. Sharp and blunt dissection is used to lyse adhesions, exposing the diaphragm musculature. A primary repair is performed using non-absorbable sutures.
A composite mesh is placed in an onlay fashion over the diaphragm for reinforcement, followed by the application of a bioabsorbable mesh to reinforce the hiatal hernia repair.
